# AI Capability Reference

> What can this AI actually do?

A plain-English reference for AI capabilities, plans, constraints, and implementations across major subscription AI products (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, Grok, Perplexity) and open models.

Maintained by SnapSynapse. Updated weekly via multi-model verification cascade.

## What this site covers

- 18 capabilities (e.g., Search the Web, Generate Images, Write and Edit Code)
- 72 implementations across 6 subscription AI products
- 9 open-model access records (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral, Codestral)
- Plan-level availability (which subscription tier unlocks what)
- Surface availability (web, desktop, mobile, terminal, API)
- Gating (free, paid, limited)
- Evidence with sources and changelog for every claim

## Machine-readable API

Stable JSON exports at: https://airef.snapsynapse.com/api/v1/

Start with the index: https://airef.snapsynapse.com/api/v1/index.json

### Available files

- capabilities.json — all capabilities with search terms, definitions, and cross-links
- products.json — all products with provider links and implementation lists
- implementations.json — all implementations with capability mappings and evidence
- providers.json — all providers with product lists
- model-access.json — model access records with deployment and constraint details
- evidence.json — evidence records with sources and changelog
- capability-matrix.json — capability x product availability grid
- product-comparisons.json — pairwise product capability overlap
- plan-entitlements.json — what each plan tier unlocks per product

### Data contract

- IDs are stable and safe to use as foreign keys
- Every record includes `verified` and `checked` dates for freshness
- File paths under /api/v1/ are stable within the version
- New fields may be added; existing fields will not be removed within v1

### Usage guidance

- Always include the `verified` date when citing data — the AI landscape changes weekly
- Respect gating and constraints — "available" does not mean "available to everyone"
- Don't strip caveats from talking points — they contain important context about restrictions
- Cache for up to 24 hours; don't poll more than once per hour
- Attribution appreciated: "Data from AI Capability Reference (https://airef.snapsynapse.com/) by SnapSynapse"

## Site pages

- https://airef.snapsynapse.com/ — Capability-first homepage (what can AI do?)
- https://airef.snapsynapse.com/implementations.html — Product-first detailed availability
- https://airef.snapsynapse.com/compare.html — Side-by-side product comparison
- https://airef.snapsynapse.com/constraints.html — Access tiers, surfaces, and limits
- https://airef.snapsynapse.com/about.html — About the project

## Bridge pages (programmatic)

### Capability checks: /can/{product}/{capability}/
Answers "Can {product} {capability}?" with plan tables, constraints, and evidence.
Example: https://airef.snapsynapse.com/can/chatgpt/search-the-web/

### Product comparisons: /compare/{product-a}-vs-{product-b}/
Side-by-side capability overlap between two products.
Example: https://airef.snapsynapse.com/compare/chatgpt-vs-claude/

### Capability landings: /capability/{slug}/
All implementations of a capability across products.
Example: https://airef.snapsynapse.com/capability/search-the-web/

### Use-case pages: /best-for/{use-case}/
Product coverage grid for a use-case category.
Example: https://airef.snapsynapse.com/best-for/creative-work/
